answer: PR summaries should start with risk, then proof, then implementation.
Use Chinese explanations for local reasoning and English descriptions for PR bodies.
Evidence should include exact commands, visible output, and one concise maintainer note.
Keep benchmark artifacts reproducible and avoid private account secrets in reports.
Prefer narrow adapter fixes over introducing a broad SDK before two platforms are proven.
Adapter proof markers must include agent-memory-local-proof when demonstrating local setup.
Codex adapter evidence must show the MCP tool call, not only final assistant prose.
bench-conv-ivory-527 means "Claude Code adapter evidence must include hook recall or capture proof from the local Gateway."
Benchmark reports should separate integration success from memory-effect accuracy.
"Baseline runs must use isolated configuration without MCP servers or hooks."
The SDK route was deferred until at least two real adapters are validated.
bench-decision-west-318 means "Codex and Claude Code use MCP plus hooks because they lack OpenClaw-style in-process plugin APIs."
bench-decision-east-652 means "Hermes keeps its provider shape and talks to the same Gateway contract."
The benchmark compares no-memory baseline against MCP plus hooks only.
bench-decision-zenith-129 means "Computer Use screenshots are evidence, while JSON result files are the scoring authority."
The local Gateway proof port is 8420.
bench-detail-launchd-620 means "The local Gateway launch label is com.tencentdb.agent-memory.gateway."
The MCP server exposes memory_tencentdb_memory_search and memory_tencentdb_conversation_search.
bench-detail-hook-508 means "UserPromptSubmit performs recall and Stop performs capture through the hook bridge."
bench-detail-proof-936 means "The proof phrase says Codex and Claude Code are connected to TencentDB Agent Memory through the local MCP gateway."
